ground_truth.title: Best Online MBA Programs
ground_truth.author: Thomas Broderick, Nalea Ko, and ACO Rankings Team
ground_truth.main_content:
Want to improve your career prospects and salary potential in the business world? Then consider applying to online MBA programs. Top MBA programs let you specialize in key business topics like finance, marketing, and management.

Full-time students can earn an MBA degree online in two years, while part-time students take around three years. No matter which option you choose, you’ll graduate with the skills needed for a successful career in business.

We ranked the best online MBA programs using criteria like tuition rates, coursework, support services, and student outcomes.

Why You Should Earn Your MBA Degree Online

Some employers require candidates for leadership and executive roles to have an MBA.
Asynchronous online MBA classes let you complete coursework on your own time.
Out-of-state public universities may offer in-state tuition rates if you enroll in a 100% online program.
The best online MBA programs feature the same student services as on-campus programs, including resume support and career services.
Online education makes it easier to network with peers living around the U.S. — or even around the world.
You can enroll in an out-of-state school without spending money on relocating.

Top Online MBA Programs

We use trusted sources like Peterson's Data and the National Center for Education Statistics to inform the data for these schools. Sponsored results from our partners appear among these rankings and are indicated as such.

#1 The University of Texas Rio Grande Valley
What Sets It Apart: UTRGV's online MBA allows you to graduate in as little as 12 months. Short, intensive courses let you focus on fewer subjects at once. The curriculum emphasizes modern, data-driven skills like business intelligence, data warehousing, and marketing analytics.
Cost per Credit:
In-State | $472
Out-of-State | $472
Credits to Graduate: 36
Programmatic Accreditation: AACSB International
Why Students Choose UTRGV:
Accelerated Timeline: The 12-month program is ideal for working adults seeking a rapid return on investment.
Analytics Emphasis: Courses in business intelligence, data warehousing, and marketing analytics build modern, data-driven skills.
No Application Fees: Apply for free with no added cost.
Know Before You Enroll:
Multiple start dates let you begin classes when it fits your schedule.
Foundation courses are available if you don’t have a business background.

#2 University of Minnesota-Twin Cities
What Sets It Apart: The University of Minnesota-Twin Cities (UNM) offers an online MBA with strong career outcomes, including solid salary increases and promotion rates. Its flexible completion window of 2-7 years lets you tailor the pace to your work and life demands. UNM also offers an in-person residency that helps you build local connections within the industry.
Cost per Credit:
In-State | $1,797
Out-of-State | $1,797
Credits to Graduate: 52
Programmatic Accreditation: AACSB International
Why Students Choose UNM Twin Cities:
Career Outcomes: UNM graduates in 2024 reported a 62% average salary increase and a 39% promotion/advancement rate within just four months.
Flexible Timeline: Complete the online MBA in 2-7 years at your preferred pace.
Built-In Residency: Connect with local employers and classmates through an in-person residency in Minneapolis-St. Paul.
Know Before You Enroll:
The program does not offer specializations, but extensive elective options allow you to customize your MBA.
You may automatically receive a 15% tuition discount if you apply by the priority deadlines or qualify through military or corporate partner status.

#3 University of Maryland-College Park
What Sets It Apart: The UMD's online MBA stands out for its AI-forward curriculum, covering topics such as machine learning, intelligent technologies, and modern global disruptions. You can also add in-person courses, certificates, and experiential learning at three Maryland/D.C. campuses, including action learning projects and opportunities to help manage a $1 million global equity fund.
Cost per Credit:
In-State | $1,733
Out-of-State | $1,733
Credits to Graduate: 54
Programmatic Accreditation: AACSB International
Why Students Choose UMD:
AI-Focused Curriculum: Learn how AI is reshaping business and build skills that give you an edge in a rapidly evolving landscape.
Specialization Options: Choose from four career-focused specializations, along with electives in AI, analytics, supply chain, and technology management.
Career Services: Access lifetime career support, including coaching, workshops, mock interviews, and recruiting tools.
Know Before You Enroll:
If admitted, you must submit a $750 deposit within four weeks to secure your seat in the program.
Merit scholarships up to $25,000 are available for spring and fall 2026 students.
The program is a combination of live online classes and self-paced learning.

#4 California State University, San Bernardino
What Sets It Apart: CSUSB's online MBA includes a unique bootcamp that helps you build key business skills before starting your core courses. The program offers true flexibility for working adults. You have the option of completing the MBA quickly with a structured 21-month plan or choosing a slower, more flexible pace.
Cost per Credit:
In-State | $1,100
Out-of-State | $1,100
Credits to Graduate: 48
Programmatic Accreditation: AACSB International
Why Students Choose CSUSB:
MBA Boot Camp: This structured set of prep modules helps you improve business fundamentals before moving onto core courses.
Focused Sessions: Two 6-week sessions per semester let you concentrate on one class at a time.
Accelerated or Flexible Path: Finish in as little as 21 months or extend your timeline as needed.
Know Before You Enroll:
This program is fully self-paced (asynchronous).
You must have at least two years of professional experience to apply.

#5 University of Utah
What Sets It Apart: The University of Utah's online MBA is designed for working professionals and delivered through a custom-built digital platform. You can stack certificates in areas such as business analytics, cybersecurity management, and information systems, or pursue dual degrees like the MHA, MSBA, or MS in finance. The program also allows you to mix online and on-campus courses for added flexibility.
Cost per Credit:
In-State | $1,500
Out-of-State | $1,500
Credits to Graduate: 48
Programmatic Accreditation: AACSB International
Why Students Choose Utah:
Stackable Credentials: Choose from five dual degree programs and four stackable certificates to increase your career potential.
Promotion Rate: About half of Utah online MBA students receive a promotion before they graduate, while 36% of graduates reported an average 36% salary increase.
Know Before You Enroll:
Expect a mix of asynchronous (self-paced) and synchronous (live) learning.
Admissions scholarships are available to newly admitted students, but they are limited to a first-come, first-served basis.

Our Methodology

At Affordable Colleges Online, we use a proprietary ranking system and data from reputable sources like the U.S. Department of Education to help you find the best school for your needs. Our most important metric — affordability — accounts for 60% of our formula on our most affordable rankings. We also consider academic quality, program offerings, online enrollment, and graduation rates.

This list has not been updated for 2025. To learn more, consult our school ranking methodology.

Application Requirements for an Online MBA

Completed application and application fee
Undergraduate transcripts
Resume
Statement of purpose
2-3 letters of recommendation
GMAT or GRE scores, if required
Interview, if required

Price Check

The average cost of an MBA is around $17,058 in tuition and fees, according to 2020 data from the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES).

Importance of Accreditation for Online MBA Programs

The best online MBA programs hold both institutional and programmatic accreditation.

You can check a school’s accreditation status by using the directories on the Council for Higher Education Accreditation website or the Department of Education website.

Top MBA programs should hold programmatic accreditation from one or more recognized business program accreditors:

Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB)
Accreditation Council for Business Schools and Programs (ACBSP)
International Accreditation Council for Business Education (IACBE)

Programs with AACSB, ACBSP, or IACBE accreditation offer many benefits, including a rigorous curriculum and high-quality student services. These programs also do the most to help you land a job.

What You’ll Study in an Online MBA Program

In an online MBA program, you’ll learn business and accounting basics. You’ll study topics like business strategy, operations management, entrepreneurship, business law, and microeconomics.

Online MBA programs require 36-48 credits across a mix of core and elective courses. You’ll also likely need to complete a capstone or thesis project.

Some programs give you the opportunity to do an in-person internship at a local business.

Most online MBA programs let you tailor your coursework to a specific concentration, such as finance or human resources.

Common MBA Concentrations

MBA concentrations give students specialized training in specific areas of business and can lead to a salary boost. You can complete an MBA in healthcare administration, entrepreneurship, finance, supply chain management, or hospitality management, among other specialties explored here.

Accounting
Entrepreneurship
Finance
Healthcare Administration
Hospitality Management
Human Resources
Information Systems
Information Technology
International Business
Marketing
Project Management
Supply Chain Management

How Much Does an Online MBA Cost?

The average MBA program charges $17,058 in annual tuition and fees, according to 2020 NCES data. Many factors affect the cost of online MBA programs, such as whether you qualify for in-state tuition.

You can lower your out-of-pocket expenses in several ways, including by applying for federal financial aid. You must submit the FAFSA each year if you want to qualify for federal grants and low-interest loans. Your school’s financial aid office can help you fill out this form accurately.

Other aid includes scholarships for MBA students.

Jobs for MBA Graduates

The jobs available for MBA graduates depend on a person’s individual concentration. For example, you may want to research programs with finance concentrations if you hope to become a finance manager.

An MBA can also help you advance your career if your employer restricts management-level positions to employees with advanced degrees. Many MBA job hubs exist to help you find a first job or start a new career. You can use these to research the latest postings for popular MBA jobs like those in the table below.

MBA Jobs and Salaries

JOB	MEDIAN ANNUAL SALARY (MAY 2024)	JOB GROWTH RATE (2024-2034)	MINIMUM EDUCATION REQUIRED
Financial Managers	$161,700	15%	Bachelor’s
Human Resources Managers	$140,030	5%	Bachelor’s
Top Executives	$105,350	4%	Bachelor’s
Financial Analysts	$101,910	6%	Bachelor’s
Project Management Specialists	$100,750	6%	Bachelor’s
Personal Financial Advisors	$102,140	10%	Bachelor’s
Management Analysts	$101,190	9%	Bachelor’s
Financial Examiners	$90,400	19%	Bachelor’s
Budget Analysts	$87,930	1%	Bachelor’s
Accountants and Auditors	$81,680	5%	Bachelor’s

Source: Bureau of Labor Statistics

Frequently Asked Questions About Online MBA Programs

How long does it take to earn an MBA?
On-campus and online MBA programs usually take full-time students about two years to finish. If you opt for an accelerated MBA, you may finish in as few as 1.5 years. Part-time students, on the other hand, typically take 3-3.5 years to earn their MBA.

Is an online MBA respected?
Employers regard accredited online MBA programs as equal to on-campus programs. Advances in online education mean the best online MBAs offer a rigorous curriculum that aligns well with employers’ needs. Note, however, that employers will still likely consider your university’s overall reputation when considering the reputability of your MBA.

Can you get a good job with an online MBA?
Yes. High-paying MBA roles include computer and information systems manager, financial advisor, and operations research analyst. These and other careers feature high median salaries, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Use your career aspirations to guide your research of online MBA programs. You may need to concentrate in a specific area to qualify for your ideal job.

How much do MBA graduates make?
MBA graduates can earn vastly different salaries depending on their years of experience, location, employer, and position. Degree-holders in leadership and management roles may earn over six figures.

According to the BLS, the median income for all business and financial occupations was $76,850 as of May 2022 — that’s around $30,000 more than the median salary for all jobs in the U.S.

Will your online MBA result in a higher salary?
Many schools report that some or most students earn more money after getting their MBA. That said, many factors can affect your earning potential, including your location, your MBA concentration, and prior work experience.

Your school’s career center can keep you up to date with changing economic trends and BLS job growth projections. The center can also help you improve your resume and interviewing skills.
